message:
I am thinking...: 
The log file modification time is `2026-05-29 10:42:01` but the current time is `10:43:50`. That's nearly 2 minutes since the log was last updated. The log size is still at 22341572 bytes. 

This is exactly the same `raddr2 [4]` region we saw before - when the previous LibreLane run deadlocked. With this run, the output goes directly to the file, but the file hasn't been updated for ~2 minutes.

Looking at the last line: `\u_core.u_cpu.core.regs.raddr2 [4] -> ... [4]` - this is bit 4 of the 5-bit register address (last bit). So this might actually be the LAST line before a phase change. The log not growing could mean:
1. Yosys is computing the next phase (ABC) and hasn't produced output yet
2. The log is being written in chunks with buffering
3. Yosys is stuck again (but this time on computation, not pipe deadlock)

With the direct-to-file output, there's no pipe deadlock. The log is written via LibreLane's internal buffering. LibreLane might buffer output internally before writing to the log file. The 2-minute gap could just be LibreLane processing the Yosys output and not yet writing the next chunk to the log file.

LR (286668) is still alive with 16.7% CPU, memory at 0.7%. Yosys subprocess should still be running. Let me check.
